  "findings": [
    "Fall protection trigger height of 6 feet was ignored during residential construction work.",
    "Employees are exposed to fall hazards greater than 6 feet without personal fall arrest systems, guardrails, or safety nets."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately in the affected elevated areas.",
    "Install compliant fall protection systems such as personal fall arrest systems (PFAS), guardrails, or safety nets before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Under OSHA 1926 Subpart M (specifically 1926.501(b)(13)), employees engaged in residential construction activities 6 feet (1.8 m) or more above lower levels must be protected by guardrail systems, safety net systems, or personal fall arrest systems. Ignoring the 6-foot trigger height is a critical safety violation that presents an immediate life-safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.501(b)(13)",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1910.140"
  ]
}
